title: lotus corniculatus subsp. alpinus
intro: Lotus corniculatus subsp. alpinus, also known as alpine bird's-foot trefoil, is a perennial legume that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its high saponin and flavonoid content. The health benefits of alpine bird's-foot trefoil include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
